name: Concora Wabash
description_short: Concora Wabash is a small federal credit union established in 1938, serving over 300 members in Wabash, Indiana with $1.4M in assets.
description_long: Concora Wabash Federal Credit Union was established in 1938 and is headquartered in Wabash, Indiana. As a not-for-profit credit union, it has deep roots in the local community and primarily serves members in the manufacturing sectors with industry-specific financial solutions. The institution operates a single branch location at 455 Factory Street and maintains a lean operational structure with over 2 dedicated employees as of 2026. The credit union offers a standard range of consumer financial products including savings accounts, share draft accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loans, auto loans (new and used vehicles), and mortgage options. Members can access online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and online bill pay services. Interest rates are publicly listed, including 3.50% for new vehicle loans, 4.50% for used vehicle loans, 12.50% for unsecured personal loans, and 0.00% for unsecured credit cards. The institution also provides financial calculators and comparative rate tools. As a not-for-profit federal credit union, Concora Wabash distinguishes itself by offering competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks, which is a defining characteristic of the credit union model. The organization has maintained continuous operation for over 85 years and serves a stable, loyal membership base. Their focus on manufacturing-sector members suggests specialized lending expertise in that industry. The primary limitation is scale: with only $1.4M in assets and 300+ members, this is an exceptionally small institution with minimal branch infrastructure (1 branch, 0 ATMs). The lobby was noted as "currently closed" on the website, raising questions about service accessibility. There are zero reviews on the platform, and the institution's limited size means fewer resources for product development or technological innovation compared to larger credit unions.
pros: Established in 1938 with over 85 years of operational history and community trust
Competitive auto loan rates (3.50% new, 4.50% used) as a not-for-profit institution
Offers online banking, mobile apps (iOS/Android), and online bill pay
Specialized lending expertise in manufacturing sectors with tailored solutions
Lower fees and better rates than for-profit banks due to not-for-profit structure
Provides mortgages, personal loans, CDs, and share certificates
Free financial calculators and rate comparison tools available
cons: Extremely small institution with only $1.4M in assets and 300+ members, limiting resources and product offerings
Single branch with zero ATMs restricts accessibility and convenience for members
Lobby status listed as 'currently closed,' raising concerns about in-person service availability
No member reviews on platform, indicating low visibility and limited member feedback data
Minimal staff (2+ employees) may limit customer service responsiveness and availability
best_for: Manufacturing sector employees seeking industry-specialized financial services
Local Wabash, Indiana residents who value community banking relationships
Individuals seeking lower fees and rates through a not-for-profit credit union model
Members comfortable with primarily digital banking (online and mobile)
diagnosis: Concora Wabash is best suited for local Wabash-area residents and manufacturing sector employees who value community banking and not-for-profit lending advantages. The main caveat is that its extremely small size (1 branch, 0 ATMs, $1.4M assets) and reportedly closed lobby severely limit accessibility and service reliability compared to mainstream credit unions or banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
